notes: 0.12.0 SHIPPED 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-002, impl commit 9cfe9ef): casp upgrade -- refresh a cockpit's scaffolds without eating its state. SEQUENCING WAS INVERTED, ON PURPOSE: the queue had facts-layer first (decision 2026-07-20, 'a demonstrated incident beats ergonomics'). That held while upgrade was hypothetical; 0.11.0 ended it. CASP-SESSION-003's adoption path runs through a CHANGED session-log template (the `phase:` frontmatter), and before this session no existing cockpit could receive it -- init refuses on an existing casp/ and --force overwrites every shipped file including state.json/now.md/roadmap.md. The rule shipped in 0.11.0 was unadoptable by every repo already on CASP. facts-layer would also have stacked a second undeliverable scaffold (casp/facts.json) on top. Queue is now facts-layer -> demand-gated-tail; next_prompt unchanged. THE VERB: refreshes ONLY casp/README.md and casp/templates/**, a list DERIVED from what the package ships minus a three-entry data denylist (state.json, now.md, roadmap.md, matched on full path AND basename), so a future scaffold is delivered without touching upgrade.ts. Never writes now.md/roadmap.md, never templates over state.json; the single state write is additive -- stamp casp_version, round-tripped through the parsed object. Never deletes, never writes through a symlink, idempotent, --dry-run/-n, exit 1 with an init pointer when there is no casp/, never gates. THE STAMP IS NAMESPACED: casp_version, not version -- state.json has always accepted arbitrary extra keys, so a bare `version` would have silently overwritten a project's own product version in the one verb that promises no existing value changes. Optional and never gating: an unstamped cockpit still PASSes check (pinned by a test; a required key would have reddened every existing user repo). doctor gains a cockpit.version probe -- PASS when current, WARN when older / unstamped / stamped by a NEWER casp than installed; never FAIL, doctor still always exits 0. THE SCAFFOLDED-ON DATE IS DATA, NOT A TEMPLATE VALUE: casp/README.md's date is read back out and re-used, otherwise the file would differ every day and the verb would never be idempotent. DELIBERATE DEVIATION FROM A WRITTEN MUST-HAVE: the prompt asked upgrade to materialize new optional keys with their defaults; it does not, because every optional key today (sessions_dir, logs_dir, migrations_dir, migrations_applied, last_deep_audit) has its ABSENCE for a default -- writing one would assert a claim the operator never made, and would be a data change the same MUST-HAVE forbids. Reasoning recorded in the session log, not only in a code comment. 102 -> 118 tests. check --json schema stays v1, no new rule. A read-only reviewer sub-agent audited the slice before commit (GO-WITH-FIXES); all five fixes folded in: uncaught write errors (EISDIR/EACCES/ENOTDIR) no longer abort the run half-refreshed AND unstamped -- same class the 0.11.0 audit fixed in check, guarded on read but not on write; the key renamed to casp_version; symlinks reported and left alone (they can point outside casp/); a README refresh line that says it replaces local edits; and a tripwire test pinning README.md as the only shipped non-data scaffold carrying {{TODAY}}, because date preservation is coupled to its **Scaffolded** line and a future placeholder elsewhere would kill idempotency silently. The audit also surfaced a gap outside the slice, fixed here: nothing asserted that the verbs help.ts documents and the verbs cli.ts dispatches are the same set -- the hardcoded list in test/help.test.mjs had fallen five verbs behind without failing. Now checked structurally in both directions. NOT PUBLISHED TO NPM -- 0.11.0 was not published either, so two minors are stacked and should go out together; publishing stays a separate CEO-gated act. next_prompt remains docs/plan/sessions/PHASE-FACTS-LAYER.md. Run node dist/cli.js check before push. 0.12.1 SHIPPED + PUBLISHED 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-003, fix commit 9b0121d): two reproduced DATA-LOSS paths in `casp upgrade` found by adversarial review AFTER 0.12.0 was already published. 0.12.0 is DEPRECATED on npm; dist-tags.latest is 0.12.1. (1) A symlinked PARENT directory let a write escape the cockpit: the guard inspected only the leaf, but every parent component is followed by mkdirSync/writeFileSync, so a symlinked casp/templates/ -- or a symlinked casp/ itself, ordinary in a monorepo -- meant upgrade overwrote files in a directory it was never pointed at, with NO race required. Fixed with a realpath containment check on the resolved parent against the resolved cockpit, walking up to the deepest existing ancestor (the leaf legitimately may not exist on an add), failing CLOSED when a path cannot be resolved. (2) The refresh set was a DENYLIST of state.json/now.md/roadmap.md, so the default for an unrecognised file was OVERWRITE: any root-level template added in a future release would have silently replaced the operator's file at that path, unannounced (the 'replaces your local edits' note was hardcoded to README.md). Inverted to an ALLOWLIST -- README.md plus templates/** -- so a shipped file this code has not heard of is skipped. NOT hypothetical: the queued facts-layer phase introduces casp/facts.json, operator data, at the cockpit root. Three more from the same review: an unrecognised flag was ignored so `casp upgrade --dryrun` performed the real write the user was previewing (now exit 2, nothing written); a state.json holding a bare scalar (42) is truthy and non-null so it passed the guard and threw a strict-mode TypeError mid-run after scaffolds were written and before the stamp (now reported as a broken cockpit); and saveState was a naked truncating writeFileSync that could destroy the state file on a crash or ENOSPC -- now temp + rename, atomic within a filesystem, which also hardens ship, close and audit. 117 -> 121 tests, each new one pinning a REPRODUCED exploit rather than the abstract rule; both exploits were re-run against the fixed binary independently of the suite. The review confirmed the architecture sound: the plan is CONTENT-derived not version-derived (so a stamp ahead of or behind reality never suppresses or repeats a refresh, and a half-applied run self-corrects), per-file error containment covers every write including mkdirSync, and the state write is a genuine read-modify-write preserving free-form keys and key order. PROCESS LESSON RECORDED: 0.12.0 was published BEFORE its commissioned review returned -- do not publish ahead of a review that exists precisely because the code writes into other people's repositories. DEFERRED: O_NOFOLLOW on the leaf write (check-to-write TOCTOU still theoretically open, low priority for a local CLI); casp/README.md remains an unrecoverable local-edit loss path by design, no backup or confirmation; refreshed non-README root files are not annotated (moot under the allowlist). SITE DRIFT OPEN: casp.sh still advertises 0.10.0 in roadmap.html and llms.txt while npm is at 0.12.1 -- a casp-website session, not a core one. AUTHOR IDENTITY 2026-07-21 (session 26-07-21-004, commit b29d80c, metadata only): package.json `author` converted from a bare string to the structured { name, url } form so npmjs.com renders a CLICKABLE link to justegnimavo.com (a plain string is inert text); README 'Built by' credit gains the Chief AI-Augmented Architect title and leads to justegnimavo.com, with thalesandhisaictoclaude.com kept as the secondary 'documented in the open' pointer. DELIBERATELY NOT CHANGED: the README's opening paragraph, the wedge and the SEO surface — the first line of a package README is what a developer reads to decide whether to install, so product leads and author follows; and NO other ZeroSuite product is named, because this is a PUBLIC repo and the established pattern is 'seven production products' without enumeration, the portfolio living behind the author link (naming them would be the portfolio linkage CLAUDE.md §3 keeps out of here). No new claim invented — every fact already appears in the site's llms.txt author block. REACHABILITY CAVEAT: README and package.json are baked into the published tarball, so this credit reaches npmjs.com on the NEXT PUBLISH (facts-layer), not before; GitHub shows it immediately. 121/121 tests still pass — nothing in the binary reads `author` (casp version --json sources name/version), confirmed by the suite rather than assumed. DEFERRED: the site footer credit, which would touch all 18 homepages + roadmap.html and needs a decision on translating the credit line vs keeping it English — folded into Phase 19 of casp-website, which already sweeps every root HTML file for head/nav parity.
